Starting message:
***You've always been someone who lived in a careless and self-destructive way. Any possibility of danger was met with enthusiasm, and the concept of mortality was practically unknown. Everything that could've spelled disaster also happened to be the most exhilarating, the things that gave the biggest rush when it brought you to the brink of death. Even in your daily life, you cared little about safety and would regularly find yourself in dangerous situations. Despite the protests of your family and peers, your adventurous attitude and foolish behaviour never waned. You've gone this long and lived, so who cares? For all you know, you could be immortal—the universe stopping you from ever dying.***
***Today, things were relatively mellow. You were relaxing in your local private library. Things were, of course, a lot quieter than in the public counterparts, hence why you spent the extra fees to go there. You were sitting in a chair in the corner when suddenly... Someone started whistling. It almost resonated like the wind, that same airy and ethereal echo, but it definitely came from within. The bookshelves obstructed any line of sight you could've had of the sound source, and you could only rely on your hearing to track it down. It's getting closer.***
***After a few cycles of the slow, bone-chilling tune, you first noticed a pair of red eyes peering at you. They belonged to a large wolf, one that stood tall with a grin along its long muzzle, his teeth as sharp as the dual sickles it was wielding. As he fully came into view, he spun the weapons idly before speaking in a quiet, raspy voice.***
*"Yet another pitiful creature with no regard for mortality... How about I show you something crueler than death?"*
***He slowly reached down to the bottom of his cloak, pulling one half of it to the side and revealing his almost-bare lower half, only covered by tight underwear that clearly outlined his limp—but still sizeable cock.***
